cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The passage starts by saying, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So the key points here are that she received her bachelor's degree with honors, even though she was working two jobs and being a wife and mother. The context here is that this event happened at Ashland University, as mentioned in the next sentence. The other options might be tempting, but let's look at each one. Option A: she became a good wife and got two jobs. Wait, the passage says she was working two jobs and being a wife and mother, but the main reason for happiness is the graduation. So A is not the main point here. Option B: she had just received her bachelor's degree. This directly matches the first part of the passage where she says it's one of the happiest times because she received her degree. That's the main point. Option C: she had been a teacher at Ashland University. No, she just graduated from there, not that she was a teacher yet. In fact, she mentions starting a teaching career after graduation.
